registry.description: Gratitude is the inner acknowledgement of gift received — recognising that what one has or is came from outside oneself and responding to that recognition with thanks. The Greek term in this registry (eucharistia) covers both the inner attitude of thankfulness and the outward expression of it. In the New Testament, gratitude is treated as one of the marks of a person rightly ordered toward God: those who have lost sight of God are characterised by ingratitude (Romans 1). Gratitude keeps the inner person from the illusion of self-sufficiency.

terms.medium_def:
expression of thanks, thanksgiving, gratitude thankfulness 
gratitude, thankfulness, Acts 24:3; thanks, the act of giving thanks, thanksgiving, 1Cor. 14:16; conversation marked by the gentle cheerfulness of a grateful heart, as contrasted with the unseemly mirth of εὐτραπελια, Eph. 5:4
terms.lsj_entry:
εὐχᾰριστ-ία, ἡ, 
thankfulness, gratitude, Decrees cited in [Refs 4th c.BC+]; πρός τινα [Refs 1st c.BC+]; πρὸς τὸν θεόν [Refs 4th c.BC+]
__2 giving of thanks, εἰς εὐ. θεοῦ [NT+1st c.AD+]
terms.short_def_mounce: expression of thanks, thanksgiving, gratitude thankfulness

terms.medium_def:
to thank, give thanks, render gratitude; this can mean words that express gratitude or the emotion of gratitude 
to thank, Lk. 17:16; absolute to give thanks, Mt. 15:36; 26:27; passive to be made a matter of thankfulness, 2Cor. 1:11
terms.lsj_entry:
εὐχᾰριστ-έω, 
bestow a favour on, oblige, τῷ δήμῳ τῷ Δηλίων [Refs 3rd c.BC+]; τινι [Refs 3rd c.BC+]
__2 to be thankful, return thanks, Decrees cited in [Refs 4th c.BC+]; τοῖς Αθηναίοις [Refs 2nd c.BC+]; ἐπί τινι or περί τινος for a thing, [Refs 2nd c.BC+]; especially to the gods, ἐπὶ τῷ ἐρρῶσθαί σε τοῖς θεοῖς εὐ. [NT+2nd c.BC+]:—passive, to be thanked, ηὐχαρίστηται κεραυνοῖς [Refs 5th c.BC+]; to be received with thanks, [NT]
__3 pray, τῷ θεῷ περί τινος [Refs 4th c.AD+]
terms.short_def_mounce: to thank, give thanks, render gratitude; this can mean words that express gratitude or the emotion of gratitude

terms.medium_def:
thankful (in word and attitude) 
grateful, pleasing; mindful of benefits, thankful, Col. 3:15*
terms.lsj_entry:
εὐχᾰριστ-ος, ον, 
agreeable, τινι τέχνη [Refs 5th c.BC+]; λόγοι [Refs]; - ότατα καὶ πιθανώτατα εἴρηκε [Refs 2nd c.BC+]; εὐχάριστα acceptable gifts, [Refs 5th c.AD+]. adverb -τως, τελευτᾶν τὸν βίον to die happily, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__II grateful, thankful, [NT+5th c.BC+] adverb -τως, διακεῖσθαι πρός τινα [Refs 1st c.BC+]
__III beneficent, θεοί [Refs 2nd c.BC+]; title of Ptolemy V, [Refs]; τὸ τῆς ψυχῆς εὐ. [Refs 1st c.BC+]; βεβαιωτὴς (-ότης codices) εὐχάριστος, of God, [Refs 1st c.AD+]
